title: Task argument inherits width of value that was passed to it
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

Run this code through yosys:
```
module test(input wire [7:0] a, output reg [8:0] b);
	task t(input wire [7:0] c, input wire d);
		b = { c, d };
	endtask
	always @* t(a, 0);
endmodule
```

## Expected behavior

I would expect the output port b[0] to be 0; and b[8:1] to equal a[7:0].

## Actual behavior

Since commit 59c4ad8 all nine bits of output port b are zero.
I suspect that the constant zero that gets passed to the second argument of the task is 32 bits wide and gets treated as 32 bit inside the task also.

## Workaround

Change `input wire d` to `input wire [0:0] d`; or change the zero constant to `1'b0`.
